_rationale: index_stats reports indexer health. C++ fix (DGeclu4miU5q): removed the parity-compat stub in src/cli/mcp.cpp:280-302 that shadowed the real handler via reverse-iteration dispatch in McpServer::handle_tools_call — stub returned hardcoded {status:indexing, file_count:0, symbol_count:1, reference_count:2, indexing_progress:25} forever. After fix, 20 sequential probes spaced 500ms apart on multi-lang corpus all return {status:ready, file_count:4, symbol_count:4, reference_count:10} (matches Go output shape). LIMITATION: this descriptor sends a single tool call with no inter-call delay. C++ indexes synchronously during `mcp` subcommand startup (src/cli/mcp.cpp:593 index_directory before MCP server loop), so the single shot reads status:ready. Go indexes asynchronously, so the same single shot reads status:indexing with mid-flight counters. Full text-body parity requires either (a) a multi-call probe descriptor that waits for ready, or (b) a normalize_mcp_inner_text rule that collapses status:indexing→ready and zeroes the mid-flight counters. Until then, text body stays in ignore tier.

tiers._rationale_stable: Envelope-only locking. Both binaries return a content[].type='text' frame, confirming the tool is wired and the real handler dispatches.
tiers._rationale_ignore: Single-shot timing mismatch: Go async indexer returns mid-flight stats, C++ sync indexer returns ready. Different shapes (Go: timestamp ISO + total_size_bytes; C++: timestamp_ms). Tighten after multi-call descriptor lands.
